and.d: a word that connects two things together
and.e: My mom and dad are going on a trip.

as.d: a word that shows how something is done or how it is
as.e: I run as fast as I can.

are.d: a word that shows more than one thing or person exists
are.e: My friends are coming over to play.

at.d: a word that shows a location or place
at.e: I'll meet you at the park.

about.d: near or around a place or thing
about.e: The cat played about the house.

also.d: Also means in addition to something else.
also.e: I like apples, and I also like oranges.

after.d: happening or coming later in time
after.e: I eat my snack after I finish my homework.

any.e: You can have any cookie you want.

am.d: The way I say I exist or I do something
am.e: I am going to the park today.

always.e: I always play outside after school.

another.e: Can I have another cookie, please?

around.e: The dog runs around the yard.

again.e: I will try the puzzle again if I can't solve it.

against.d: in opposition to or in contact with
against.e: The cat jumped against the window to get outside.

away.e: I went away to visit my grandparents.

already.e: I already finished my homework for the day.

anything.e: I can play with anything in my room.

area.d: A part of a place or thing
area.e: The park has a nice grassy area to play.

actually.e: I actually saw a bird outside the window.

american.d: related to the United States of America
american.e: I live in an American city.

able.d: Having the skill or opportunity to do something.
able.e: The young artist is able to paint very well.

air.e: I like to play outside in the fresh air.

almost.d: very close to but not quite
almost.e: I almost finished my homework before bedtime.

among.d: In the middle of or surrounded by a group
among.e: The kittens were playing among the toys in the living room.

act.d: To pretend to be someone or something else
act.e: The actors had to act like they were superheroes in the play.

ago.e: I saw that movie a long time ago, when I was younger.

along.d: with or next to something else
along.e: I walked along the path to the park.

although.d: even though or despite the fact that
although.e: Although it was raining, we still went outside to play.

ask.d: to say something in order to get an answer
ask.e: Can you please ask the teacher for help?

according.e: According to the map, the park is this way.

age.d: the number of years someone has lived
age.e: I am 8 years old, which is my current age.

anyone.e: Anyone can join the school club.

action.d: Something you do to make something happen
action.e: She took action to help her friend in trouble.

asked.d: To say something in order to get an answer
asked.e: I asked my teacher for help with the project.

april.d: The fourth month of the year

art.d: Creative work, like painting or drawing
art.e: I really enjoy learning about different kinds of art.

above.e: The bird is flying above the trees.

available.d: Something that is ready to be used
available.e: The library has many books available to borrow.

across.d: Going from one side to the other
across.e: The cat ran across the street to the other side.

account.d: A record of money that you have or owe
account.e: I put my allowance in my savings account each week.

al.e: I like cookies and cake, but not al ice cream.

alone.e: The puppy was left alone in the house.

added.d: To put more things together to make a bigger amount.
added.e: The baker added more flour to the dough.

america.d: A very big country with 50 states that many people live in.
america.e: The family went on a trip across America to see the different states.

answer.d: The correct response to a question or problem.
answer.e: The student wrote the answer to the math problem on the board.

average.d: Something that is typical or normal
average.e: My test score was about average for the class.

areas.d: Parts or sections of a larger place
areas.e: The park has many different areas for playing and relaxing.

amount.d: How much or how many of something there is
amount.e: I have a small amount of money in my piggy bank.

attention.d: When you focus on something carefully.
attention.e: The student pays attention during the lesson.

add.d: To put two or more numbers or things together.
add.e: The teacher asked the students to add 5 and 7.

army.d: A large group of soldiers who fight together.
army.e: The army protected the city from the enemy.

attack.d: To try to hurt or damage something or someone.
attack.e: The dog attacked the cat in the yard.

article.d: A piece of writing about a particular topic
article.e: I read an interesting article about space in my magazine.

access.d: The ability to enter or use something.
access.e: I have access to the school library to borrow books.

allowed.e: My parents allowed me to stay up late on my birthday.

august.d: A very important and special month
august.e: August is the last summer month before school starts.

africa.d: A large continent south of Europe
africa.e: My family is taking a trip to Africa next summer.

allow.e: My parents allow me to stay up late on the weekends.

association.d: a group of people who have the same interests
association.e: The soccer association organizes games for all the local teams.

australia.d: a country that is an island continent
australia.e: Many kangaroos and koalas live in the country of Australia.

amazing.d: Something that is very impressive or wonderful
amazing.e: The sunset was amazing, with beautiful colors in the sky.

addition.d: Something that is added to something else
addition.e: In addition to apples, the fruit salad had oranges and grapes.

ahead.d: Ahead means in front of or further along.
ahead.e: I see the finish line ahead of me in the race.

album.d: An album is a collection of songs, photos, or other items.
album.e: My favorite singer just released a new music album.

ability.d: the power to do something well
ability.e: My friend has a great ability to draw pictures.

analysis.d: the careful study of something to understand it better
analysis.e: The scientist did an analysis of the experiment results.

approach.d: A way to do or get something
approach.e: I need to find the best approach to finish my homework.

anti.e: She is anti-bullying and wants to stop it.

active.d: being physically busy and moving around
active.e: The kids were active and played outside all day.

announced.d: To tell everyone about something new
announced.e: The teacher announced that we have a field trip today.

administration.d: The group of people who manage or lead an organization
administration.e: The school administration makes important decisions for the students.

additional.d: More than what is usual or expected
additional.e: I need to buy some additional supplies for my art project.

anymore.d: not any longer or any more
anymore.e: I don't play with toys anymore, I'm a big kid now.

asking.e: The teacher is asking the students to raise their hands.

authority.d: someone who is in charge and makes the rules
authority.e: The principal is the authority at our school.

awesome.e: Winning the game was an awesome feeling!

activity.d: Something you do for fun or to be active.
activity.e: The summer camp has many fun activities for the kids.

annual.d: Something that happens once a year.
annual.e: The school holds an annual talent show every spring.

anyway.e: I'm hungry anyway, so let's get a snack.

associated.e: My new shoes are associated with the blue outfit I'm wearing.

absolutely.e: I absolutely love playing outside on sunny days.

activities.d: things you do for fun or to learn
activities.e: I love all the fun activities at summer camp.

agree.d: to say 'yes' or to have the same idea
agree.e: The kids all agreed to go to the park today.

address.d: the location of a building or house
address.e: The package was delivered to the correct address.

african.e: The African elephant is the largest land animal.

agency.d: a company or organization that provides a service
agency.e: The travel agency helped plan my family's vacation.

airport.d: a place where planes take off and land
airport.e: We waited at the airport for our flight to take off.

australian.e: The Australian kangaroo is a unique animal.

author.d: a person who writes books or stories
author.e: The famous author wrote a new book that everyone wanted to read.

avoid.d: to stay away from or not do something
avoid.e: I try to avoid eating sweets before dinner.

advice.d: Suggestions or recommendations about what to do.
advice.e: My parents give me good advice about my homework.

agreed.d: When two or more people have the same opinion.
agreed.e: The class agreed to have a party on Friday.

agreement.d: A decision or plan that two or more people make together.
agreement.e: The students and teachers made an agreement about the school rules.

animals.d: Living things like dogs, cats, and birds.
animals.e: My favorite animals are the monkeys at the zoo.

accept.d: To agree to take or receive something
accept.e: I will accept the prize if I win the contest.

appear.d: To come into sight or seem to be present
appear.e: The new restaurant will appear in our town next month.

appears.d: To seem to be true or real
appears.e: It appears that it will rain later today.

aircraft.d: A vehicle that can fly, like a plane or helicopter.
aircraft.e: The aircraft flew high in the sky.

authorities.d: People or groups in charge of a place or situation.
authorities.e: The authorities said the park is closed today.

appeared.e: The lost puppy suddenly appeared in the yard.

attempt.e: She will attempt to climb the tall tree.

actual.e: The actual flower in the vase was so pretty.

arms.d: The two body parts that are connected to your shoulders
arms.e: She reached out her arms to give her mom a hug.

actions.d: Things you do or make happen
actions.e: The hero's brave actions saved the day.

animal.d: A living thing like a dog, cat, or bird
animal.e: The zoo had many different kinds of animals.

arrived.d: To reach a place or destination
arrived.e: The bus finally arrived at the school after the long drive.

aware.e: The child was aware of the new rules.

advantage.d: Something that helps you do better
advantage.e: Having a good plan gives you an advantage in the game.

aid.d: Help or support that someone gives you
aid.e: The nurse gave the patient first aid when they got hurt.

apparently.d: It seems to be true, but you're not sure
apparently.e: Apparently, it's going to rain today.

award.d: A prize given for doing something well
award.e: The student won an award for their excellent science project.

application.d: A form you fill out to ask for something.
application.e: I need to complete my application for school.

acting.d: Pretending to be a different person or character
acting.e: The actor was great at acting like a superhero.

apart.e: The friends sat apart from each other at the party.

adult.d: A grown-up person, not a child
adult.e: My mom is an adult who works hard every day.

affairs.d: The things that a person is involved with or responsible for
affairs.e: The teacher is in charge of all the school's affairs.

apply.d: To put in an effort to get something you want
apply.e: I will apply for the job by sending in my resume.

agent.d: A person who acts on behalf of another person or organization
agent.e: The real estate agent helped my family find a new home.

alive.e: The baby squirrel is alive and healthy.

applied.d: To use something for a specific purpose
applied.e: The student applied her math skills to solve the problem.

artist.d: A person who creates paintings, drawings, or other art
artist.e: The talented artist painted a beautiful landscape.

assembly.d: A group of people gathered together for a common purpose
assembly.e: The school held a special assembly to celebrate the new year.

automatically.d: Something that happens by itself without being told to do it
automatically.e: The sprinklers turn on automatically every morning.

ah.d: An expression of surprise or realization.
ah.e: Ah, I just remembered what I needed to do today.

ancient.d: Very old, from a long time ago.
ancient.e: The ancient ruins were discovered deep in the jungle.

accepted.d: When something is agreed to or approved.
accepted.e: My drawing was accepted into the art show.

accident.d: Something that happens by mistake, not on purpose.
accident.e: I spilled my milk by accident.

accounts.d: A record of money coming in and going out.
accounts.e: The store keeps careful accounts of all their sales.

americans.d: People who live in the United States of America.
americans.e: Many Americans enjoy going to the beach.

arm.d: The part of your body from your shoulder to your hand.
arm.e: I use my arm to wave to my friend.

ad.d: a short message that tries to get you to buy something
ad.e: The store has a new ad for their summer sale.

afraid.d: feeling scared or worried about something
afraid.e: The little girl was afraid of the dark.

advanced.d: Being more skilled or developed than most
advanced.e: The advanced math class is for students who are really good at numbers.

afternoon.d: The part of the day that is after noon and before evening
afternoon.e: My favorite part of the day is the quiet time in the afternoon.

alternative.d: A different choice or option instead of the usual one
alternative.e: If the movie theater is too crowded, the alternative is to watch a movie at home.

appreciate.d: To be thankful for and value something

appropriate.d: Suitable or right for a particular situation
appropriate.e: Wearing a swimsuit is appropriate for going to the beach, but not for school.

allows.d: To let something happen or give permission

appeal.d: To try to get someone to agree with you
appeal.e: The politician made an appeal to the audience to vote for them.

approximately.e: There are approximately 30 students in the classroom.

audience.d: The people watching or listening to something
audience.e: The audience clapped when the performers came on stage.

acts.d: Things that people do or things that happen.

affected.d: When something changes or influences someone or something.

appearance.d: How someone or something looks on the outside.

apple.d: A round, juicy fruit that grows on trees.
apple.e: My mom baked an apple pie for dessert.

asia.d: The largest continent in the world, where countries like China and India are located.
asia.e: My family is taking a trip to Asia this summer.

anywhere.e: I can play with my toys anywhere in the house.

apartment.d: A home inside a larger building with other homes
apartment.e: My family lives in a cozy apartment.

argument.d: A disagreement or debate about something
argument.e: The two friends had a short argument about the rules of the game.

armed.d: Having weapons or tools to use for protection or fighting
armed.e: The police officer was armed with a badge and a gun.

attacks.d: When someone tries to hurt or damage something
attacks.e: The dog attacks the ball when we play fetch.

advance.d: To move forward or get better at something.
advance.e: She was able to advance to the next level of the game.

angry.d: Feeling mad or upset about something.
angry.e: The little boy was angry when his toy broke.

articles.d: Small words like 'the', 'a', or 'an' that we use in sentences.
articles.e: The cat played with a ball and the toy mouse.

artists.d: People who create paintings, drawings, or other types of art.
artists.e: The artist painted a beautiful picture of the park.

arts.d: Creative work like painting, music, or dance.
arts.e: She likes to spend time doing arts and crafts.

awards.d: Special prizes given to people for doing something well.
awards.e: The teacher gave the student an award for their hard work.

adding.e: I am adding more blocks to my tower.

angeles.d: a big city in California, USA
angeles.e: Los Angeles is a very busy city.

app.d: a small computer program you can use on your phone
app.e: I have a fun game app on my tablet.

adopted.d: When you become the new parent of a child.
adopted.e: The family adopted a new baby and gave her a loving home.

answers.d: The right response to a question
answers.e: I raised my hand to give the answer to the math problem.

appointed.d: To choose someone for a job or position
appointed.e: The principal appointed the new student council leader.

arrested.d: When the police take someone into custody
arrested.e: The police officer arrested the person who broke the law.

asian.e: My family is from an Asian country.

attitude.d: The way a person thinks or feels about something

affect.e: The rain will affect how long we can play outside today.

allowing.d: to let something happen or be done
allowing.e: My parents are allowing me to stay up later tonight.

assistant.d: a person or thing that helps you do a task
assistant.e: My teacher's assistant helps me with my schoolwork.

applications.d: Applications are forms you fill out to ask for something, like a job or a loan.
applications.e: I need to complete my college applications before the deadline.

approved.d: Approved means something was accepted or allowed.
approved.e: My parents approved my plan to have a sleepover with my friends.

assistance.d: Assistance is help or support that someone gives you.
assistance.e: I asked my teacher for assistance with my math homework.

assume.d: Assume means to believe something is true, even without proof.
assume.e: I assume my sister is at the park with her friends.

achieve.d: To achieve means to successfully complete something you wanted to do.
achieve.e: The student achieved her goal of getting good grades.

actor.d: An actor is a person who pretends to be someone else in a play or movie.
actor.e: The famous actor walked down the red carpet.

alex.d: Alex is a name that can be used for a boy or a girl.
alex.e: My friend Alex has a pet dog.

adam.e: Adam was the boy who won the race.

admit.d: To say that something is true or that you did something.
admit.e: The student had to admit that they broke the window.

adults.d: Grown-up people who are not children.
adults.e: The adults in the family helped decorate the house.

arrest.d: When the police catch someone who did something wrong.
arrest.e: The police had to arrest the person who stole the bike.

aside.d: Something that is put to the side or not the main thing.
aside.e: I'll put that idea aside for now and focus on this one.

agents.d: People who do special jobs to help other people.
agents.e: The travel agents helped us plan our vacation.

anybody.e: Anybody can play outside on a sunny day.

accurate.e: The map had accurate information about the roads.

achieved.e: The student achieved good grades this semester.

administrative.d: Relating to the management or organization of something
administrative.e: The administrative staff at the school are very helpful.

anniversary.d: A special day that marks an important event that happened in the past.
anniversary.e: My mom and dad celebrate their wedding anniversary every year.

aspects.d: Different parts or features of something.
aspects.e: The new park has many great aspects, like a playground and a soccer field.

assets.d: Things that someone or something owns that have value.
assets.e: The company has many valuable assets, like buildings and computers.

ages.d: The number of years someone or something has been alive or has existed.

alright.d: Something that is okay or good enough.
alright.e: The test was alright, not too easy or too hard.

attempts.d: When you try to do something.
attempts.e: The student made several attempts to solve the math problem.

attend.d: To go to an event or place.
attend.e: I will attend the birthday party this weekend.

academic.d: relating to school subjects and learning
academic.e: Studying hard is important for my academic success.

accused.e: The teacher accused the student of cheating on the test.

afford.d: to be able to pay for something
afford.e: I can't afford to buy that toy right now.

agencies.d: a company or organization that provides a service
agencies.e: The travel agency helped me plan my vacation.

aim.d: to try to do or get something
aim.e: The basketball player aimed the ball at the hoop.

attached.d: connected or joined to something else
attached.e: The drawing was attached to the refrigerator with a magnet.

attractive.e: The colorful flowers in the garden are very attractive.

andrew.e: Andrew played soccer with his friends.

attorney.d: A person who helps people with legal problems.
attorney.e: The attorney helped the family with their case.

advertising.d: telling people about a product or service to try to get them to buy it
advertising.e: The toy company used advertising to let people know about their new toys.

attacked.d: when someone tries to hurt or damage something
attacked.e: The dog attacked the squirrel when it got too close.

absolute.e: The rule is absolute, no exceptions.

agricultural.d: Having to do with farming and growing crops
agricultural.e: The farm had agricultural equipment for planting.

arab.d: A person from the Middle East region
arab.e: The Arab family moved to a new country.

atlantic.d: The name of a big ocean
atlantic.e: The Atlantic Ocean is on the east side of the United States.

atmosphere.d: The air all around the Earth
atmosphere.e: The atmosphere protects us from the sun's rays.

audio.e: The video had great audio quality.

attended.d: To be present at an event or activity
attended.e: The students attended the school play last night.

absence.d: When someone or something is not there
absence.e: The teacher noticed my absence from class today.

academy.d: a school that teaches special skills
academy.e: The basketball academy trains young players to become great athletes.

agriculture.d: the science of growing crops and raising animals
agriculture.e: Farming is a type of agriculture that provides us with food.

authors.d: people who write books, stories, or articles
authors.e: The authors of this book spent a lot of time writing it.

aged.e: The aged wooden chair had been in the family for generations.

alliance.d: When two or more groups work together to help each other
alliance.e: The two teams made an alliance to win the tournament.

amounts.d: The total number or quantity of something
amounts.e: The recipe calls for small amounts of sugar and salt.

angel.d: A special kind of person who helps and protects others
angel.e: The firefighter was an angel who saved the cat from the tree.

approval.d: When someone agrees that something is good or okay
approval.e: The teacher gave her approval of the student's art project.

argue.d: When people disagree and try to convince each other they are right
argue.e: The siblings argued about who got to watch TV first.

assessment.d: A test or way to check how well someone has learned something
assessment.e: The teacher gave an assessment to see what the class had learned.

attempted.d: When someone tries to do something, even if they don't succeed
attempted.e: The student attempted to solve the math problem.
